CBSL Sri Lanka Canary

Central Bank of Sri Lanka — IMF Conditionality & Recovery Intelligence

Domain Number: #84
Cluster: 5 — Economics & Business
Region: SOUTH ASIA / Indian Ocean
Currency: LKR (Sri Lanka Rupee)
Issued: 2026-07-05
Generated: 2026-07-18 19:00 UTC

Executive Summary

Sri Lanka's LKRI stands at 0.4650 (WATCH) as of Q3 2026. The island nation remains in a fragile recovery following the 2022 sovereign default — the largest and most studied EM sovereign stress case of the decade. The IMF Extended Fund Facility (EFF) programme ($2.9B, March 2023) is the central pillar of recovery, and CBSL conditionality compliance is the primary signal this canary monitors.

Five kill conditions drive the LKRI: KC_01 IMF Programme Compliance (weight 0.30, the dominant signal), KC_02 LKR/FX reserve adequacy, KC_03 political stability under the Dissanayake (AKD/NPP) government, KC_04 tourism revenue trajectory, and KC_05 external debt restructuring progress — where China's $5B bilateral debt remains the critical unlock.

All five KCs are in WATCH zone. No single condition has crossed into ELEVATED. The key risk vector is a combination of KC_01 fiscal slippage and KC_05 China bilateral stall, which would directly threaten IMF disbursement continuity.

2022 Sri Lanka Default — Case Study

The Most Studied EM Sovereign Stress Case of the Decade

Sri Lanka's 2022 default ($51B external debt) is the defining EM sovereign stress event of the 2020s. The CBSL canary exists specifically because Sri Lanka's trajectory provides the clearest IMF conditionality compliance signal in the EM universe.

April 2019 — Easter Sunday Bombings
Easter Sunday terrorist attacks killed 269, devastating tourism which contributed ~12% of GDP. Arrivals collapsed 18% in 2019 and set the vulnerability trajectory.
2020-2021 — COVID Collapse
COVID wiped out tourism entirely (arrivals: 2.33M → 0.19M). Foreign exchange reserves began a sustained collapse as tourism, remittances, and exports fell simultaneously.
March 2022 — FX Reserves Below $1B (Usable)
The critical threshold: usable FX reserves fell below $1B, triggering payment capacity collapse. Government unable to finance fuel, medicine, food imports. 13-hour power cuts nationwide.
May 2022 — Sovereign Default
Sri Lanka defaulted on $51B of external debt — first default in the nation's post-independence history. USDLKR hit 370+.
July 2022 — Aragalaya / Gotabaya Resignation
Mass protests (Aragalaya — "struggle") stormed the presidential residence. President Gotabaya Rajapaksa fled and resigned. Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe became President.
March 2023 — IMF EFF Approved ($2.9B)
IMF Extended Fund Facility ($2.9B, 48-month programme) approved. First disbursement $0.33B. Programme conditionality: fiscal surplus targets, tax reform, SOE restructuring, LKR flexibility.
October 2024 — Dissanayake (AKD/NPP) Elected
Anura Kumara Dissanayake (NPP/JVP) won presidential election on anti-corruption platform. IMF programme continuity confirmed but implementation friction with new government's socialist-leaning base.
2025-2026 — Recovery (Fragile)
FX reserves recovered to ~$5.5B. Tourism recovering. GDP returned to positive growth. But IMF conditionality compliance, China debt bilateral, and political stability remain WATCH signals.

External Debt Restructuring Tracker

International Bondholders
~$12.5B
⚠ WATCH — ISB restructuring ongoing

International Sovereign Bonds. Discount terms under negotiation. 35% of total external debt.

China (Bilateral)
~$5B
⚡ RISK — Most complex creditor

China Exim Bank / CDB exposure. Paris Club and IMF tranches partially blocked pending China resolution. KC_05 primary driver.

IFIs (IMF/WB/ADB)
~$8B
✓ STABLE — Preferred creditor

Preferred creditor status. Not subject to restructuring. IMF EFF is the recovery mechanism.

Japan
~$3.5B
✓ ALIGNED — Paris Club

Japan aligned with Paris Club framework. Waiting for China comparability of treatment.

India
~$1.5B
✓ COOPERATIVE

India provided critical swap lines during 2022 crisis ($4B). Bilateral cooperation ongoing. Strategic relationship.

Other Bilateral
~$20B
⚠ WATCH

Mixed bilateral creditors. Paris Club framework covers most. Resolution dependent on China comparability trigger.

Tourism Recovery Status

2.33M
2018 Peak Arrivals
0.19M
2021 COVID Low
~2.2M
2026e Arrivals
~12%
Tourism % pre-COVID GDP

Key tourist source markets: India (~30%), China (~10%), UK (~8%), Germany (~5%), Russia (~5%), Maldives transfer traffic.

Risk precedents: Easter Sunday bombings (April 2019) caused immediate -18% arrival drop and lasting travel advisory drag. Any security incident in Sri Lanka would trigger KC_04 escalation rapidly.

Current status: No travel advisories in force. India-Sri Lanka ferry service (Rameshwaram-Talaimannar) boosting connectivity. Hotel occupancy Colombo recovering to ~70%. Southern circuit (Galle, Mirissa) performing strongly.
